verses.text: The psalm pleads God to remember David's afflictions and his vow that he would not rest until he found 'a place for the LORD.' His zeal for God's house was wholehearted. David longed to honour God before he sought his own ease.

The psalm pleads God to remember David's afflictions and his vow that he would not rest until he found 'a place for the LORD.' His zeal for God's house was wholehearted. David longed to honour God before he sought his own ease.

They recall finding the ark and resolve, 'We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship,' praying the priests be clothed with righteousness. Worship is their joy. The people gather eagerly to the place of God's presence.

They recall finding the ark and resolve, 'We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship,' praying the priests be clothed with righteousness. Worship is their joy. The people gather eagerly to the place of God's presence.

For David's sake they plead, recalling the LORD's sworn oath, 'Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.' God's promise to David is sure. It points beyond Solomon to Christ, the everlasting King of David's line.

For David's sake they plead, recalling the LORD's sworn oath, 'Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.' God's promise to David is sure. It points beyond Solomon to Christ, the everlasting King of David's line.

For David's sake they plead, recalling the LORD's sworn oath, 'Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.' God's promise to David is sure. It points beyond Solomon to Christ, the everlasting King of David's line.

The LORD has chosen Zion for His dwelling: 'This is my rest for ever,' and He will bless her provision and clothe her priests with salvation. God delights to dwell among His people. Where He chooses to rest, He pours out abundant blessing.

The LORD has chosen Zion for His dwelling: 'This is my rest for ever,' and He will bless her provision and clothe her priests with salvation. God delights to dwell among His people. Where He chooses to rest, He pours out abundant blessing.

There He will make 'the horn of David to bud' and a lamp for His anointed, while clothing his enemies with shame. The royal line will flourish. God exalts His chosen King and confounds all who oppose Him.
